LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ESPETUS CHURRASCARIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL RELEASE AND PRODUCTION OF PLAINTIFF'S DISABILITY RECORDS ON FILE WITH THE EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS - DEFENDANTS SOUGHT TO SUBPOENA PLAINTIFF'S "PERSONAL INFORMATION" FROM THE CALIFORNIA EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT. (SEE CIV. CODE 1798.3(A) ("'PERSONAL INFORMATION' MEANS ANY INFORMATION THAT IS MAINTAINED BY AN AGENCY THAT IDENTIFIES OR DESCRIBES AN INDIVIDUAL").) THE EDD REFUSED TO PRODUCE THE INFORMATION. DEFENDANTS THEN FAILED TO BRING A MOTION TO ENFORCE THE SUBPOENA WITHIN 20 DAYS OF SERVICE OF THE EDD'S WRITTEN OBJECTION, RENDERING THE SUBPOENA MOOT. (SEE CCP 1985.3(G); CCP 1985.4 ("WITNESS" DEFINED AS STATE AGENCY; "CONSUMER" INCLUDES STATE EMPLOYEES).) GIVEN THAT THE SUBPOENA IS MOOT, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH IT IS ALSO MOOT. NO SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO ANYONE. THE ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE ORDER FOR FULL DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: MTORREANO@SFTC.ORG. =(302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TAMMY VANNORSDALL'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENA OF UNEMPLOYMENT AND DISABILITY RECORDS AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND THEIR COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $4,960.- DEFENDANTS SOUGHT TO SUBPOENA PLAINTIFF'S "PERSONAL INFORMATION" FROM THE CALIFORNIA EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT. (SEE CIV. CODE 1798.3(A) ("'PERSONAL INFORMATION' MEANS ANY INFORMATION THAT IS MAINTAINED BY AN AGENCY THAT IDENTIFIES OR DESCRIBES AN INDIVIDUAL").) THE EDD REFUSED TO PRODUCE THE INFORMATION. DEFENDANTS THEN FAILED TO BRING A MOTION TO ENFORCE THE SUBPOENA WITHIN 20 DAYS OF SERVICE OF THE EDD'S WRITTEN OBJECTION, RENDERING THE SUBPOENA MOOT. (SEE CCP 1985.3(G); CCP 1985.4 ("WITNESS" DEFINED AS STATE AGENCY; "CONSUMER" INCLUDES STATE EMPLOYEES).) GIVEN THAT THE SUBPOENA IS MOOT, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH IT IS ALSO MOOT. NO SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO ANYONE. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE ORDER FOR FULL DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TAMMY VANNORSDALL'S Motion For Court Approval Of Settlement And Dismissal Of The Paga Action.

Plaintiff Tammy Vannorsdall's motion for court approval of settlement and dismissal of PAGA action is granted. The Court approves the Settlement Agreement to the extent that it provides for the dismissal without prejudice of Plaintiff's PAGA causes of action, without any associated release of claims, in exchange for a mutual release of associated costs; thus, the Settlement Agreement will not prejudice the rights of other aggrieved employees. To the extent that the Settlement Agreement includes a release of Plaintiff's individual claims in exchange for other, unspecified consideration, court approval is not required.

The Court will execute the proposed order included with the moving papers.

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Shannon Seibert, Esq. of Seibert Bautista Montoya, 510.679.1981, appearing for the Plaintiff Tammy Vannorsdall;
Charles J. Antonen, Esq, of Dept. of Justice - Office of the Attorney General, 415.703.3500, appearing for Third Party Employment Development Department;
Natalie A. Pierce, Esq. of Littler Mendelson PC, 415.288.6321. appearing for the Defendant Espetus Churrascaria Inc.;
Gal Gressel, Esq. of Littler Mendelson PC, 415.677.3175, appearing for the Defendant Espetus Churrascaria Inc.

DEFENDANT ESPETUS CHURRASCARIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL RELEASE AND PRODUCTION OF PLAINTIFF'S DISABILITY RECORDS ON FILE WITH THE EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPARTMENT, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS.

Matter called, ARGUMENT held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Defendants sought to subpoena plaintiff's "personal information" from the California Employment Development Department. (See Civ. Code 1798.3(a) ("'personal information' means any information that is maintained by an agency that identifies or describes an individual").) The EDD refused to produce the information. Defendants then failed to bring a motion to enforce the subpoena within 20 days of service of the EDD's written objection, rendering the subpoena moot. (See CCP 1985.3(g); CCP 1985.4 ("witness" defined as state agency; "consumer" includes state employees).) Given that the subpoena is moot, plaintiff's motion to quash it is also moot. No sanctions are awarded to anyone. The order is signed in open court. (see order for full details)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-05-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances are made by or for the parties.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

RULING: Defendants Epetus Churrascaria, Inc. and Paulo Klein's demurrer to the first, fourth, sixth and seventh causes of action for discrimination, harassment, retaliation, and Labor Code violations in the complaint filed by plaintiff Tammy Vannorsdall is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all four causes of action. As written in the complaint, all four causes of action are uncertain because more than one claim is alleged or at least arguably alleged in each cause of action. Ms. Vannorsdall must allege each claim as a separate cause of action. Discrimination and harassment based on different protected characteristics are distinct claims. Likewise, violations of different retaliation statutes and different provisions of the Labor Codes are distinct claims.

Order signed.
